one_line_ask: Audit the outsourced editor's Voltcast episode deliverables -- the 16:9 YouTube master and the 9:16 vertical cut -- against the channel's written delivery-spec sheet, and return a timestamp-referenced QC findings report (as a report PDF plus structured JSON) listing every spec drift: duration, resolution/fps, loudness, music-vs-narration balance, missing intro/outro, unsafe-margin overlays and off-brand text.
brief:
Voltcast is a fictional independent consumer-tech review channel run by Voltcast Media that publishes several times a week -- long-form reviews plus short vertical cuts for Shorts / Reels / TikTok. We outsource the actual editing to a rotating pool of junior editors, and before an episode goes live we run a delivery QC pass so nothing off-spec reaches the feed (the last episode published with the sponsor read buried under the music and the price-callout overlay clipped off the bottom on mobile, and we only caught it after it went out).

For this episode ('Voltcast E42 -- Budget Phone Shootout') you will receive four things: (1) voltcast_e42_master_16x9.mp4, the finished 4K 16:9 YouTube master the editor delivered; (2) voltcast_e42_vertical_9x16.mp4, the reformatted vertical Shorts/Reels cut derived from the same edit; (3) voltcast_delivery_spec.pdf, our written channel delivery-spec sheet; and (4) voltcast_brand_frames.png, a reference sheet of our correct intro end-card, lower-third, and price-callout overlay so you can compare on-screen text against the approved look. The delivery-spec sheet states the exact rules: long-form runtime must land in the 8:00-12:00 window; master must
